key_insight: AITutor shifts AI math support from giving answers to helping students inspect, repair, and verify reasoning under exam pressure.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests UI and interaction designs—layered examples, visual grounding, and metacognitive prompts—that can make AI tutors more usable and pedagogically safer in high-stakes learning settings.
method: Generative design study to translate pedagogy into interface features
Usability study of student interactions with the AITutor system
evidence_signal: The evaluation combines 7,379 telemetry events, 8 contextual observations, and 10 interviews from junior-high Zhongkao preparation use cases.
watch_item: The findings come from a small field deployment in a high-pressure exam context, so broader generalization and long-term learning effects still need validation.
paper_title: From Answer Generators to Reasoning Facilitators: Designing AI Tutors for Mathematical Reasoning in High-Stakes Environments
